wikitext:
{{Chess Opening Theory/Position |Modern variation |eco=[[Chess/ECOB|B12]] |parent=[[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...c6|Caro-Kann]] → [[../|2...d5]] }} == 3. Nd2 · Modern variation == White prepares to recapture on e4 with their knight while keeping the option of c3. This move is intended to avoid the Gurgenidze system, [[../3. Nc3|3. Nc3]] [[../3. Nc3/3...g6|g6]], where Black will fianchetto their king's side bishop. Here, [[/3...g6|'''3...g6''']] may be answered 4. c3, mitigating the pressure Black's bishop will be able to impose on the long diagonal. This encourages the main move, [[/3...dxe4|'''3...dxe4''']], and after 4. Nxe4 this [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...c6/2. d4/2...d5/3. Nc3/3...dxe4/4. Nxe4|transposes into the same line]] as if 3. Nc3 dxe4 4. Nxe4 had been played. == Theory table == {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}} '''1.e4 c6 2.d4 d5 3.Nd2'''
3 4 5 6
